title: Yearning for Nostalgia
body:
I want to be a kid again
but when I was a kid all I wanted was to be old—
to drive a fast car,
to buy takeout when I wanted.

now when I close my eyes,
the memories begin to replay—
I spend every minute wishing I could
run under the big colorful parachute one more time,
to ride those square scooters one more time,
to jump rope for hearts,
to run away from smoke I swore I'd never touch.

I want to be a kid again—
to feel that innocent happiness,
no FAFSA, no late fees, no student loans,
no what's next,
no seasonal depression that doesn't know when the seasons change.

if I can't go back,
let me keep a piece of that wonder I once had—
the dreams to go out and change the world,
the gall to be different.

let me be a kid one more time,
at least in my mind.
